name: Practically No-Fat Chocolate Souffle Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line the bottom of an 8-inch springform pan with parchment paper, creating a 2-3/4-inch high side.
3. Lightly grease the pan and parchment with vegetable oil spray.
4. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons sugar evenly over the prepared surface.
5. Finely grind walnuts and 2 tablespoons sugar in a food processor until finely ground.
6. Transfer the nut mixture to a large bowl.
7. Add the remaining sugar, cocoa powder, and vegetable oil to the bowl with nuts.
8. Using an electric mixer, beat egg whites and salt until soft peaks form.
9. Gradually fold the beaten egg whites into the cocoa mixture in three additions.
10. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
11. Bake for about 30 minutes or until the cake puffs up slightly and a tester inserted into the center comes out with moist crumbs attached.
12. Allow the cake to cool in the pan for about 30 minutes, then carefully remove from the pan and allow it to cool completely on a wire rack.
13. Sprinkle powdered sugar over the cooled cake.
A rich cake based on a French-countryside recipe from restaurant consultant Rozanne Gold. Made without butter, and with cocoa powder instead of chocolate, the cake is like a souffle that rises, then sinks as it cools.
